     The Numbers Business Report

Borderlands (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $15,482,668Details
International Box Office $17,455,628
Worldwide Box Office $32,938,296
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $536,937
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,171,509
Total $1,708,446
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Lilith, an infamous treasure hunter with a mysterious past, reluctantly returns to her home planet of Pandora to find the missing daughter of Atlas, the universe’s most powerful S.O.B. She forms an unexpected alliance with a ragtag team of misfits—Roland, once a highly respected soldier, but now desperate for redemption; Tiny Tina, a feral pre-teen demolitionist; Krieg, Tina’s musclebound, rhetorically challenged protector; Tannis, the scientist who’s seen it all; and Claptrap, a persistently wiseass robot. These unlikely heroes must battle alien monsters and dangerous bandits to find and protect the missing girl, who may hold the key to unimaginable power. The fate of the universe could be in their hands—but they’ll be fighting for something more: each other.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,601,572 (55.6% of total gross)
Legs:1.80 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:47.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$115,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,125 opening theaters/3,125 max. theaters, 2.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $16,372,475

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 9th, 2024 (Wide) by Lionsgate
August 9th, 2024 (IMAX) by Lionsgate
International Releases: August 7th, 2024 (Wide) (Philippines)
August 7th, 2024 (Wide) (Colombia)
August 7th, 2024 (Wide) (Iceland)
August 7th, 2024 (Wide) (Israel)
August 7th, 2024 (Wide) (Switzerland (French Speaking))
Video Release: August 30th, 2024 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of violence and action, language and some suggestive material.
(Rating bulletin 2839 (cert #53512), 6/19/2024)
Running Time: 102 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Filming location (country): Hungary
Subgenre: Action Comedy
Source:Based on Game
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Arad Productions, Picturestart, Gearbox, 2K, Lionsgate
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Lilith, an infamous treasure hunter with a mysterious past, reluctantly returns to her home planet of Pandora to find the missing daughter of Atlas, the universe’s most powerful S.O.B. She forms an unexpected alliance with a ragtag team of misfits—Roland, once a highly respected soldier, but now desperate for redemption; Tiny Tina, a feral pre-teen demolitionist; Krieg, Tina’s musclebound, rhetorically challenged protector; Tannis, the scientist who’s seen it all; and Claptrap, a persistently wiseass robot. These unlikely heroes must battle alien monsters and dangerous bandits to find and protect the missing girl, who may hold the key to unimaginable power. The fate of the universe could be in their hands—but they’ll be fighting for something more: each other.
